name: The Great Silence
description: A special tool designed to neutralize aurogen-based energy sources. Developed by Neue Zukunft as some kind of "fire extinguisher" capable of initiating an emergency shutdown of aurogen reactors that have gotten out of control. Thanks to the efforts of Kaiser's Friends, it was transformed into a futuristic anti-reactor rifle.

name: Anti-Reactor Cell
description: Specialized energy cell for The Great Silence anti-reactor rifle. Discharges a focused pulse that forces an emergency shutdown of an aurogen reactor that has gotten out of control.

name: Crystal
description: A rare crystal – it must be worth something.

name: Backpack
description: Your adventure companion: holds everything from guns to treasures!

name: Binoculars
description: The Binoculars are one of the pieces of Player Gear carried by all explorers of Sophie. They can be used to see across far distances and are useful for scouting out locations as well as keeping a lookout for other players and Tramplers.

name: Black Box
description: Incredibly valuable proof of your victory over someone.

name: NZ Mk1 Modified Energy Rod
description:
A main energy source. A standardized capsule with aurogen that fits most reactors and motor-reactors.
Makes your trampler go really fast for a short time thanks to the modernization of the valve system, which gives the motor-reactor a short burst of extra energy right after the rod is activated.

name: NZ Mk2a Enriched Energy Rod
description:
A main energy source. A standardized capsule with aurogen that fits most reactors and motor-reactors.
Makes your trampler go pretty fast for a short time thanks to the improved structure of the aurogen, which gives the motor-reactor a short burst of extra energy right after the rod is activated.

name: Time Bomb
description: An incredibly serious argument that can open a lot of doors for you. Make sure to activate it before running away.

name: Canned Sea Deer XL
description: An XL-sized can of the famous Sea Deer, an exotic fish discovered by Sophie's first colonists.

name: S&H Captain's Compartment
description: One of the most common souvenirs brought by tourists from Sophie, it is a fairly valuable resource required in the production of some items.

name: Crowns
description: The main currency here. Has a familiar face on it.

name: Crate of Shells
description:
The Crate of Shells is a Loot Container that can be found all across Sophie. Just like all containers, it has 3 tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare.
All crates contain one type of Ammunition for mounted weapons and Fabric Scraps within. Rare crates additionally contain a second type of ammunition and Metal Rods within. Very rare crates contain all three type of ammunition and Reinforced Leather Strips additionally within.

name: Raw Aurogen Crystal
description: A fragment of a strange crystal, ready for transport. Caution – radiation hazard.

name: District Officer's Portable Safe
description: A safe that contains something very valuable that belonged to the District Officer. Considering how bureaucratic the Austro-Hungarians are, it's probably a bunch of documents.

name: Dreadnaught
description:
The Dreadnaught is a Landmark that can only be found in the game mode [[storm-dive]], and holds some of the best loot in the game.

Spawning
Unlike other landmarks, the dreadnaught does not spawn at the start of the game, instead it has a rare chance of spawning during the in game event Final Extract

The dreadnaught is the exclusive home of experimental weapons and items, along with some of the rarest materials in the game.

name: NZ Mk2 Energy Rod
description: Your main source of energy. Put it in your reactor to keep the trampler working.

name: Ficus
description: It's a living plant!

name: Flare Gun
description: The Flare Gun is one of the pieces of Player Gear carried by all explorers of Sophie. It is able to shoot colored flares high into the sky, where they burst into a colored cloud depending on the flare that is loaded. Flares deal no damage and do not burst unless shot upwards, although they leave a smaller smoke trail of their color in their wake regardless of how they are fired. Flares are useful for signaling to teammates and other Tramplers.

name: Flashlight
description: The Lamp is one of the pieces of Player Gear carried by all explorers of Sophie. It can be toggled on and off by clicking on the item inside the player's inventory. When on, the lamp provides limited illumination in front of the player, which can be useful during the night when visibility is poor. Note: other players are able to see the illumination provided by your lamp, so keep an eye on your surroundings.

name: Canned Food
description: The amount of food on board the trampler determines the number of quick respawns available for the Crew. You also can just eat it to improve your well-being.

name: Food Crate
description:
The Food Crate is a Loot Container that can be found all across Sophie, most commonly at city and port Landmarks.
Just like all containers, it has 3 "Rarity" tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare. Normal crates contain Canned Food and Threads within. Rare and very rare crates additionally contain Leviathan Meat OR Coral Dust and Reinforced Leather Strips within, respectively.
Crates also contain a hidden "Quality" tier that is primarily location driven based on where the crate spawns at.
Both the rarity and quality of a crate impact how much of each item the crate contains within.

name: Box with Radio Beacon
description: This container is valuable!

name: Box with Radio Beacon
description: This container is valuable!

name: Box with Radio Beacon
description: This container is valuable!

name: Black Key
description:
Opens something marked with black. Looks important.
The tag text is decorated with military emblems and states: "Kaiser Fleet's Flagship, The Great _________, Admiral's Quarters".

Use it to open Dreadnaught (Storm Dive Exclusive).

name: Blue Key
description:
Opens something marked with blue, but what exactly?
The barely distinguishable text on the tag states the following: "Franz's S_o_age _ut, Me_e__s__g_ fi__in_ vi_l__e".

Can be found behind the Green Door.

name: Fort Storage Room Key
description: All district officers had this master key. Can open storage rooms in almost any fort.

name: Green Key
description:
Opens something marked with green, but what exactly?
The barely distinguishable text on the tag states the following: "Chi_f Dis_r_ct Ac_o_nt_nt's Office, K__s__pl__ isla_d".

name: Red Key
description:
Opens something marked with red, but what exactly?
The barely distinguishable text on the tag states the following: "C__ef I__er_al M__it_ry A_chi__ct's Offi_e, S___n isl__d".

Can be found behind the Blue Door.

name: White Key
description:
Opens something marked with white, but what exactly?
The barely distinguishable text on the tag states the following: "F__t I__ri_ Dis_ri_t Of__e_'s Wor___o_p".

Can be found behind the Red Door.

name: Box Key
description: Looks suitable for a small lock, like those used on chests or boxes.

name: Rusty 40 mm Autocannon Kit
description:
Some ingenious engineers at the station decided that this twin-barreled anti-aircraft cannon would work perfectly fine against ground targets as well. Because of the old barrel design, the cannon may overheat and jam if it shoots consecutively for too long.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Due to long-term storage in adverse conditions, it is slightly rusted, but can still be used for its intended purpose.

Utilizes 40 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Auto Turret T1 Container

name: Worn 40 mm Autocannon Kit
description:
Some ingenious engineers at the station decided that this twin-barreled anti-aircraft cannon would work perfectly fine against ground targets as well. Because of the old barrel design, the cannon may overheat and jam if it shoots consecutively for too long.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Has signs of active use.

Utilizes 40 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Auto Turret T2 Container

name: Pristine 40 mm Autocannon Kit
description:
Some ingenious engineers at the station decided that this twin-barreled anti-aircraft cannon would work perfectly fine against ground targets as well. Because of the old barrel design, the cannon may overheat and jam if it shoots consecutively for too long.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the factory.

Utilizes 40 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Auto Turret T3 Container

name: Experimental 40 mm Autocannon Kit
description:
A modified trigger mechanism, experimentation with materials and a little barrel cooling system wizardry, and a regular twin-barreled anti-aircraft gun gained the ability to increase its combat rate of fire from its own heating during firing. Be careful: watch the temperature!
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the laboratory.

Utilizes 40 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Auto Turret T4 Accelerating Container

name: Rusty 70 mm Shotgun Cannon Kit
description:
A cannon designed for buckshot shells that disperse many small projectiles when fired. The armor plate mounted on the front of the turret will protect the cannon operator from most gunfire. It is highly lethal against infantry at close range. The projectiles are solid enough to deal some damage to structures as well.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Due to long-term storage in adverse conditions, it is slightly rusted, but can still be used for its intended purpose.

Utilizes 70 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Shotgun Turret T1 Container

name: Worn 70 mm Shotgun Cannon Kit
description:
A cannon designed for buckshot shells that disperse many small projectiles when fired. The armor plate mounted on the front of the turret will protect the cannon operator from most gunfire. It is highly lethal against infantry at close range. The projectiles are solid enough to deal some damage to structures as well.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Has signs of active use.

Utilizes 70 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Shotgun Turret T2 Container

name: Pristine 70 mm Shotgun Cannon Kit
description:
A cannon designed for buckshot shells that disperse many small projectiles when fired. The armor plate mounted on the front of the turret will protect the cannon operator from most gunfire. It is highly lethal against infantry at close range. The projectiles are solid enough to deal some damage to structures as well.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the factory.

Utilizes 70 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Shotgun Turret T3 Container

name: Experimental 2x70 mm Twin Shotgun Cannon Kit
description:
One gun is already good, but two is always better. An experimental twin 70 mm shotgun cannon, created by SGOW as an attempt to increase firepower and salvo weight without significantly increasing the dimensions of the turret. Named "Eight" internally for its specific appearance. Manufactured in a limited batch for field testing.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the factory.

Utilizes 70 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Shotgun Turret T4 Double Barrel Container

name: Rusty 80 mm Naval Cannon Kit
description:
A naval cannon stripped from Sophie's military fleet tramplers by scavengers. Covers great distances but is quite hard to operate due to its slow rate of fire.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Due to long-term storage in adverse conditions, it is slightly rusted, but can still be used for its intended purpose.

Utilizes 80 mm shells.

name: Worn 80 mm Naval Cannon Kit
description:
A naval cannon stripped from Sophie's military fleet tramplers by scavengers. Covers great distances but is quite hard to operate due to its slow rate of fire.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

Has signs of active use.

Utilizes 80 mm shells.

name: Pristine 80 mm Naval Cannon Kit
description:
A naval cannon stripped from Sophie's military fleet tramplers by scavengers. Covers great distances but is quite hard to operate due to its slow rate of fire.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the factory.

Utilizes 80 mm shells.

name: Experimental 80mm Cannon Kit
description: ##Placeholder##
derivedName: Game Packed Turret T4 Double Barrel Container

name: Experimental 80 mm Railgun Kit
description:
A naval cannon, retrofitted in the Neue Zukunft laboratories with an electromagnetic accelerator. It consumes a huge amount of energy, but allows you to forget about ballistics – even standard 80 mm shells receive such a high initial velocity that the cannon literally shoots "on target" at any distance.
A complete set of spare parts and tools for field assembly and disassembly.

In perfect condition, as if fresh from the laboratory.

Utilizes 80 mm shells.
derivedName: Game Packed Turret T4 Rail Gun Container

name: HG-6 Contact Grenade
description: A single-use hand grenade – or a reusable mace. Fragile. Handle with care.

name: Podorozhnyk Bio-Emitter
description: A portable medical station. When activated, generates a healing field capable of mending wounds and restoring health. A prototype. Short-term action. Will not protect against fatal injuries.

name: KF-B "Hole" Middling Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF-B "Trench" Great Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF "Kormylo" Wheelhouse
description: The helm stripped from the civilian vessel and mounted on the Trampler Deck. One of the basic modifications that Kaiser's Friends Gang can provide for the Scavengers that work with them.

name: KF-L "Abyss" Royal Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF-L "Hole" Middling Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF-L "Trench" Great Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF "Nest" Framed Armored Turret Deck
description: The lower support of a gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. Engineers from the Kaiser's Friends gang additionally equipped it with a supporting frame, which allows other compartments to be installed on it, and armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: KF "Nest" Framed Turret Deck
description: The lower support of the gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. Engineers from the Kaiser's Friends gang additionally equipped it with a supporting frame, which allows other compartments to be installed on it.

name: KF "Prolomnyk" Battering Ram
description: The bow section stripped from the icebreaker. You might ask whether a tropical planet had ice. Who knows?

name: KF-Q "Abyss" Royal Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF-Q "Trench" Great Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: KF-Q "Well" Small Chassis
description: Kaiser's Friends mechanics found out that the chassis can function perfectly well without its central section. However, it takes a few more materials to hold it together. They made sure to fully utilize this newfound discovery on every chassis variation they could lay their hands on.

name: Map
description: The Map is one of the pieces of Player Gear carried by all explorers of Sophie; Tramplers also come equipped with a map mounted to the central steering column of the steering deck. The map takes the form of old nautical charts from back when Sophie still had liquid water on its surface. As this is no longer the case, maps now contain little useful information beyond the locations of Landmarks such as ports and military forts — the rest is out of date, shrouded in a fog of war. As the player traverses the dried-up sea floors of Sophie they slowly fill in the chart to reflect the land's new topography, and updates are reflected across both the player's personal map and the map of any Trampler they own. Because landmarks and terrain are randomly generated for each lobby, any progress filling in a map is lost upon extraction; every expedition begins with a fresh map obscured by fog of war, except for the player's immediate surroundings at spawn.

name: MedKit
description: Whether it's a critical wound or a small scratch, the MedKit will fix it in no time.

name: Medical Cabinet
description:
The Medical Cabinet is a Loot Container which can be found all across Sophie, most commonly at city and port Landmarks. Just like all containers, it has 3 tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare.
Just like all containers, it has 3 "Rarity" tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare. Normal cabinets contain Medkits and Mixtures within. Rare and very rare cabinets additionally contain Fabric and Optic Lenses within, respetively.
Cabinets also contain a hidden "Quality" tier that is primarily location driven based on where the cabinet spawns at.
Both the rarity and quality of a cabinet impact how much of each item the cabinet contains within.

name: Multitool
description: The Repair Tool, or Multitool, is one of the pieces of Player Gear carried by all explorers of Sophie. It can be used in or out of battle to quickly repair damage to doors, compartments, pipes, and hatches on your Trampler.

name: NZ AzE80 Motor-Reactor, Covered (1x3)
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable. This model is enclosed in a solid compartment to protect it from any debris falling on the reactor during construction work.

name: NZ AzE80 Motor-Reactor, Covered (2x2)
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable. This model is enclosed in a solid compartment to protect it from any debris falling on the reactor during construction work.

name: NZ AzE81 Motor-Reactor, Armored
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable. This model features additional armor plates installed at Seearsenal Neues Triest factories, allowing this compartment to be installed on Imperial military tramplers.

name: NZ AzE81L Deck Motor-Reactor, Rounded
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable.

name: NZ AzE81S Deck Motor-Reactor, Long
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable.

name: NZ AzE81S Motor-Reactor, Framed Long
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable. It has been further modified by Kaiser's Friends to be able to carry other compartments on top of the frames installed on this compartment.

name: NZ AzE82 Motor-Reactor, Armored
description: The heart of the trampler. Provides power to all compartments of the trampler and its chassis. Created by Neue Zukunft, the motor-reactor converts aurogen from energy rods into huge amounts of energy. Destroying the reactor will cause a chain reaction throughout the whole trampler, detonating most of its compartments and rendering it completely inoperable. This model features additional armor plates installed at Seearsenal Neues Triest factories, allowing this compartment to be installed on Imperial military tramplers.

name: NZ Mb2k Maneuver Engine, Small
description: Specifically designed for small-sized tramplers operating in narrow places. Despite its small size, it is still able to moderately improve chassis maneuverability. It doesn't require an individual exhaust pipe.

name: NZ Mb3 Maneuver Engine, Medium
description: Used on heavy-duty transporters to improve chassis maneuverability when transporting extremely heavy loads. This model is increased in size and features a built-in exhaust pipe. These modifications are able to provide a considerable boost to chassis performance.

name: Old Smuggler's Jacket
description: The leather is sturdy enough to save you from a shot or two.

name: SGOW SW.52 Body Armor
description: A well-worn breastplate, quilted jacket, and stylish belt – it immediately becomes clear that the wearer is not only ready for battle, but has already been through many.

name: SGOW SW.73 Light Armor
description: The standard armor of combat sailors and field officers of the Kaiser's fleet. The breastplate can reflect even the sun's rays. In it, you will be the most stylish Galician <s>marauder</s> scientist of all! And definitely the most resilient.

name: Orbital Strike Pointer
description: The portable sighting complex "Zeigefinger" of the orbital surveillance system "Bärenjäger" (aka "Berehynia"), developed by Neue Zukunft in cooperation with SGOW to help pioneers explore new worlds. Rumor has it that the system is still present in the orbit of Sophie.

name: Parts Crate
description:
The Parts Crate is a Loot Container that can be found all across Sophie, mainly at Landmarks.
Just like all containers, it has 3 "Rarity" tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare. Normal Parts crates mainly store Mechanical Parts and Scrap Metal along with a chance to contain Mk2 Energy Rod within. Rare and very rare crates additionally contain Gunpowder and HG Gunpowder, respectively.
Crates also contain a hidden "Quality" tier that is primarily location driven based on where the crate spawns at. Normal and greater crates additionally contain Pneumatic Components within.
Both the rarity and quality of a crate impact how much of each item the crate contains within.

name: 8x21 mm Ammo
description: Basic ammo used by most small firearms. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 8x21 mm FMJ Ammo
description: Special ammo used by most small firearms. Its full metal jacket makes it highly effective against armor. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 8x21 mm Incendiary Ammo
description: Special ammo used by most small firearms. Contains highly flammable materials inside. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 8x21 mm HV Ammo
description: Special high-velocity ammo used by most small firearms. Its increased powder charge makes bullets fly faster compared to standard ammunition. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 8x21 mm Toxic Ammo
description: Special ammo used by most small firearms. Contains highly toxic materials provided by Sophie's Molfars.

name: Pestkop Lorenz Amplifier
description: A portable amplifier station. When activated, it generates an electromagnetic field. Metal objects that have started moving inside the field receive an additional charge, which, for example, significantly increases the damage from bullets and shells. Initially developed to protect against harmful radiation, in practice, it has also proven useful in a somewhat opposite way.

name: Von Liebig Reflector
description: Deflects projectiles in the direction it was pointed when activated. The result of experiments involving aurogen in the mirror-manufacturing process.

name: Domovyk Protective Dome
description: A portable protection station. When activated, generates a magnetic field designed to slow or even stop any physical objects moving into it at high velocity. Won't stop a human or a trampler, but works well against harsh environmental conditions such as bullets and projectiles. Included in the standard set of pioneer equipment.

name: Repair Kit
description: If you've noticed that your trampler has more holes than it has walls, this is the right tool to use.

name: M82 Rifle
description: The SGOW M82 9 mm revolver rifle. Highly versatile like a Swiss Army knife. Quite slow when it comes to reloading.

name: KF866/9R "Mehrzel" Repeater
description: A field modification of a 9 mm breechloader by Kaiser's Friends as an attempt to convert a mass-produced single-shot musket into a magazine-fed rifle. Although a fairly successful modification, it was not widely used due to its excessive complexity.

name: Alloy Steel
description:
The latest development in the world of metallurgy, this steel with the addition of alloying elements allows it to withstand much higher temperatures and be more resistant to physical damage. It was thanks to this development that Ironclad tramplers gained their ability to land directly from orbit.
Other metals simply wouldn't be able to withstand such temperatures and stress from a fall.

name: Coral Dust
description: Rare subspecies of local coral, additionally cleaned and ground into powder. Widely used in military and pharmaceutical fields.

name: Coral Chunk
description: One of the most common souvenirs brought by tourists from Sophie, it is a fairly valuable resource required in the production of some items.

name: Fabric
description: Usually a common material, but not in this case: over the years, most local fabric items have either crumbled to dust or are beyond salvage.

name: Fabric Scraps
description: Various pieces of fabric and leather, mostly remnants of clothing, tents, carpets, curtains and even sails, left long ago in the harsh desert climate. However, some look quite new.

name: Gunpowder
description: Standard military gunpowder. Not of the best quality due to cheap components, nevertheless sufficient for mass production. Used for most conventional military ammunition.

name: High-Grade Gunpowder
description: The new smokeless gunpowder recipe, discovered on Sophie. The Imperial forces combined their regular gunpowder with coral dust, resulting in a powder that lasted longer and produced much less smoke.

name: Leviathan Meat
description: The lump of meat of a mysterious creature that lurks beneath the sands. But where's the carcass?

name: Leviathan Skin
description: A piece of skin that, by its texture, is reminiscent of a snake's skin but, by the looks of it, this piece belonged to something way bigger that a snake.

name: Scrap Metal
description: Various parts – anything made from metal – gathered into one pile.

name: Metal Rods
description: High-quality metal forged into rods – ready for use and easy to transport.

name: Mechanical Parts
description: Commonly used parts in heavy-duty machinery.

name: Pneumatic Parts
description: Crucial elements in more sophisticated machinery.

name: Computing Module
description: These parts were used in the most technological marvels created by the brightest minds of the Empire.

name: Mixtures
description: Various mixtures, syrups, tinctures and pills that were widely available for practically anyone on Sophie.

name: Optic Lenses
description: These lenses were used everywhere: from rifle and gun sights to eyeglasses and binoculars. Due to their fragile structure, there are not many left on Sophie, so they are considered very valuable.

name: Reinforced Leather Strips
description: Strips of leather sewn with fabric are a fairly durable material used to make uniforms, shoulder pads, and body armor for imperial troops.

name: Scrapped Ammo
description: This ammo will likely blow up in your hands or won't work at all if you try to use it. But the materials from it can still be salvaged and repurposed.

name: Threads
description: A basic spool of thread used by anyone.

name: Weapon Parts
description: Receivers, spare barrels, gas tubes and other replaceable parts of regular military weapons.

name: Weird Coral
description: A weirdly shaped coral that emits some brightly colored fumes, similar to those often seen on Upiórs. Inhaling these fumes would probably not be the best idea...

name: EB "Vita" Police Revolver
description: A modified 8 mm revolver manufactured by Eisenblut, used by the Sophie Gendarmerie. Received a six-shot drum compatible with speed loading.

name: EB "Tiszt" Award Revolver
description: A special modification of the 8 mm revolver manufactured by Eisenblut, used as decoration for personal service to the state, courage and high professionalism. In addition to artistic decoration, the revolver also received an eight-shot drum compatible with speed loading.

name: EB "Zseb" Revolver
description: One of the cheapest 8 mm revolvers you can find on the grey market. Production – Eisenblut; sales – virtually any arms dealer.

name: EB "Bantam" Revolver
description: A six-shot 8 mm revolver made by Eisenblut, improved by Kaiser's Friends for very close combat encounters.

name: 9x42 mm Ammo
description: Ammo that is used by most infantry rifles. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 9x42 mm FMJ Ammo
description: Special ammo for basic infantry rifles. Its full metal jacket makes it highly effective against armor. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 9x42 mm Incendiary Ammo
description: Special ammo for basic infantry rifles. Contains highly flammable materials inside. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 9x42 mm HV Ammo
description: Special high-velocity ammo for basic infantry rifles. Increased powder charge makes bullets fly faster compared to standard ammunition. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 9x42 mm Toxic Ammo
description: Special ammo for basic infantry rifles. Contains highly toxic materials inside. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: M1866/9 "Einzel" Breechloader
description: Shoot, reload, repeat. A very cheap and affordable breech-loading rifle, chambered for 9 mm cartridges. Another link in the evolution of firearms from muzzle-loading muskets to modern rifles.

name: M82 "Aperture" Rifle
description: The SGOW M82 9 mm revolver rifle with a modified aperture sight. Highly versatile like a Swiss Army knife. Quite slow when it comes to reloading. Mounted with the special sights that allow the shooter to cover longer distances with increased accuracy.

name: RW1 Rocket Launcher
description: The Raketenwerfer 1 is one of the secret prototypes built by the Kaiser's SGOW engineers on Sophie. Unfortunately, it never reached Earth -– the entire batch was "lost" during the mass evacuation.

name: Armor-Piercing Rocket
description: An unofficial rocket for the RW1 rocket launcher, handmade by someone very resourceful. Packs a punch, but is ineffective against infantry.

name: High-Explosive Rocket
description: An unofficial rocket for the RW1 rocket launcher, handmade by someone very resourceful. Not effective against armor, but capable of inflicting significant damage to infantry in a fairly large radius.

name: S&H Armaments Workbench
description: Such workshops are an essential part of warships, where they are used to maintain the crew's weapons and ammunition. It has been refitted by scavengers into a full-fledged armory to allow the construction of weapons and ammunition from even the most primitive components found on the planet during expeditions. You might ask, "Why didn't they just use it on the space station?" Well, you can check the Saint Cristopher Station's "Rules of Habitat and Machinery Maintenance" Chapter 5, Article 32, Lines 24 to 32.

name: S&H Armaments Workshop
description: Such workshops are an essential part of warships, where they are used to maintain the crew's weapons and ammunition. It has been refitted by scavengers into a full-fledged armory to allow the construction of weapons and ammunition from even the most primitive components found on the planet during expeditions. You might ask, "Why didn't they just use it on the space station?" Well, you can check the Saint Cristopher Station's "Rules of Habitat and Machinery Maintenance" Chapter 5, Article 32, Lines 24 to 32.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 77B-L Small Chassis
description: Used by small construction tramplers owned by Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG. Tramplers built on this chassis are capable of operating in hard-to-reach places due to their compact design and high maneuverability. However, load capacity is not among their strong points.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 77B-Q Small Chassis
description: Used by small construction tramplers owned by Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG. Tramplers built on this chassis are capable of operating in hard-to-reach places due to their compact design and high maneuverability. However, load capacity is not among their strong points.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 79H-B Great Chassis
description: Made by Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG at the request of Sophie's government. It was designed for heavy-duty transporters that carried spacecraft parts. Much less maneuverable compared to smaller models but can withstand extremely high loads.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 79H-L Great Chassis
description: Made by Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG at the request of Sophie's government. It was designed for heavy-duty transporters that carried spacecraft parts. Much less maneuverable compared to smaller models but can withstand extremely high loads.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 79H-Q Great Chassis
description: Made by Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG at the request of Sophie's government. It was designed for heavy-duty transporters that carried spacecraft parts. Much less maneuverable compared to smaller models but can withstand extremely high loads.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 83U-B Middling Chassis
description: Maintains the perfect balance between maneuverability and carrying capacity. Chassis of this type, due to their extremely versatile design, have been used in many areas, from construction tramplers and cargo carriers to quick and agile military reconnaissance models.

name: S&H Atm.Fs 83U-L Middling Chassis
description: Maintains the perfect balance between maneuverability and carrying capacity. Chassis of this type, due to their extremely versatile design, have been used in many areas, from construction tramplers and cargo carriers to quick and agile military reconnaissance models.

name: S&H Auxiliary Corridor
description: A compartment that can be installed to improve the trampler's ergonomics by creating more protected passages between functional compartments. Quite fragile.

name: S&H Balcony, Extended L&R
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment on both sides to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S&H Balcony, Extended Left
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment leftwards to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S&H Balcony, Extended Right
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment rightwards to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S&H Balcony, Inner Corner (L)
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This model can be installed in the inner corner between compartments.

name: S&H Balcony, Inner Corner (R)
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This model can be installed in the inner corner between compartments.

name: S&H Bridge Balcony
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile. This particular model acts as a small bridge, allowing it to cover a 1x1 gap.

name: S&H Canopy Deck
description: A simple set of decks covered with a canopy. Protects your head from the harsh heat of Sophie or anything that falls from above.

name: S&H Captain's Cabin
description: A crew compartment specially designed for the trampler's captain. The standard equipment includes a personal refrigerator and reinforced armor. The cabin also houses the Trampler Ownership Device, "IO". Crew access to doors and other trampler functions is granted and overwritten exclusively through this device.

name: S&H Cargo Bay, L-Shape
description: A cargo compartment that was used to transport a large number of various goods during long journeys before the cataclysm. Large and sturdy, it can hold quite a lot of things, which is why it is very heavy.

name: S&H Cargo Bay, Parallel
description: A cargo compartment that was used to transport a large number of various goods during long journeys before the cataclysm. Large and sturdy, it can hold quite a lot of things, which is why it is very heavy.

name: S&H Cargo Bay, U-Shape
description: A cargo compartment that was used to transport a large number of various goods during long journeys before the cataclysm. Large and sturdy, it can hold quite a lot of things, which is why it is very heavy.

name: S&H Cargo Compartment, Medium
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S&H Cargo Compartment, Small
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S&H Cargo Compartment, Small L-Shape
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S&H Cargo Deck
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. An inexpensive option, the increase in capacity comes at the cost of no protection from explosions or burglars.

name: S&H Cargo Deck, Framed
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. An inexpensive option, the increase in capacity comes at the cost of no protection from explosions or burglars.

name: S&H Cargo Deck, Framed L-Shape
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. A cheaper version with increased capacity achieved through a complete lack of protection.

name: S&H Cargo Deck, Framed Light
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during trampler deployment and evacuation. An inexpensive option, at the cost of no protection from explosions or burglars. A lighter version of the regular cargo deck that has only one rack with shelves.

name: S&H Cargo Deck, L-Shape
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. A cheaper version with increased capacity achieved through a complete lack of protection.

name: S&H Cargo Deck, Light
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during trampler deployment and evacuation. An inexpensive option, at the cost of no protection from explosions or burglars. A lighter version of the regular cargo deck that has only one rack with shelves.

name: S&H Compact Armaments Workshop
description: Such workshops are an essential part of warships, where they are used to maintain the crew's weapons and ammunition. It has been refitted by scavengers into a full-fledged armory to allow the construction of weapons and ammunition from even the most primitive components found on the planet during expeditions. Installed in a more compact compartment for better ergonomics. You might ask, "Why didn't they just use it on the space station?" Well, you can check the Saint Cristopher Station's "Rules of Habitat and Machinery Maintenance" Chapter 5, Article 32, Lines 24 to 32.

name: S&H Deck, Beveled (1x1)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Beveled (1x2)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Round (1x1)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Round (2x1)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Round-Beveled
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Standard
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments.

name: S&H Deck, Standard (no railings)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics, it provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. It can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments. This particular model comes with the railings removed.

name: S&H Entrance Vestibule
description: A crucial part of any trampler. The ladder significantly eases the process of climbing onto the trampler. A fully enclosed entrance with a vestibule provides enhanced security measures. This compartment cannot be installed on top of other compartments or chassis, as the access hatch with the ladder is mounted in its lower deck.

name: S&H Fortified Entrance Area
description: A crucial part of any trampler. The ladder significantly eases the process of climbing onto the trampler. Solid walls and roof not only increase protection, but also allow for the installation of additional compartments on top.

name: S&H Framed Stairs
description: Stairs attached to a frame, making the traversal between trampler levels easier. This model provides more space for walking and is a bit sturdier than mounted stairs.

name: S&H Lower Corner Supporting Frame (L)
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H Lower Corner Supporting Frame (R)
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H Lower Supporting Frame
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H Lower Supporting Frame Extended (L)
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H Lower Supporting Frame Extended (L&R)
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H Lower Supporting Frame Extended (R)
description: Supporting frames that were designed to be mounted on walls to support decks and other structures above them. They were often used on Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG tramplers to save space and make them more maneuverable on construction sites.

name: S&H M78 Framed Steering Deck
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn! This version has improved ergonomics and allows you to install other compartments on top of it.

name: S&H M78k Flybridge
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one compact module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn!

name: S&H M78k Framed Steering Deck
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn! This version is more compact, has improved ergonomics and allows you to install other compartments on top of it.

name: S&H M78l Flybridge
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn!

name: S&H M78r Flybridge
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn!

name: S&H M78r Framed Steering Deck
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn! This version has improved ergonomics and allows you to install other compartments on top of it.

name: S&H MK1 Crew Cabin, Single
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days. The compact model is designed for personal use.

name: S&H MK2 Crew Cabin, 2 People
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days.

name: S&H MK4 Crew Cabin, 4 People
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days. This particular design features increased room capacity and can sustain up to four crew members.

name: S&H Mounted Stairs, Left
description: Designed to improve the trampler's ergonomics by allowing easier movement between trampler levels. Was commonly used by builders on "Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG" tramplers during colonization.

name: S&H Mounted Stairs, Right
description: Designed to improve the trampler's ergonomics by allowing easier movement between trampler levels. Was commonly used by builders on "Sternenwerft Hofbauer GmbH & Co. KG" tramplers during colonization.

name: S&H Standard Balcony
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Lightweight but fragile.

name: S&H Supporting Frame
description: An additional load-bearing frame that allows the placement of decks without blocking the space below them. More lightweight than solid compartments but more fragile.

name: S&H Supporting Frame, Triangle
description: An additional load-bearing frame that allows the placement of decks without blocking the space below them. More lightweight than solid compartments but more fragile.

name: S.Trs "Albrecht" Royal Chassis
description: Specially Designed by Seearsenal Neues Triest engineers for the "Albrecht" Military Trampler, a trampler named after Franz Joseph's nephew. It loses in maneuverability to any civilian model but can literally carry a military fort on itself.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony, Extended L&R
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment on both sides to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony, Extended Left
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment leftwards to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony, Extended Right
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire. This model extends beyond the dimensions of the standard compartment rightwards to allow for connection to additional balconies.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony, Inner Corner (L)
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire. This model can be installed in the inner corner between compartments.

name: S.Trs Armored Balcony, Inner Corner (R)
description: One of many ways to improve the trampler's ergonomics. Designed to facilitate movement around the compartments. Equipped with light armor that will protect against infantry weapons but will not withstand cannon fire. This model can be installed in the inner corner between compartments.

name: S.Trs Armored Deck
description: Platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics. Provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. Equipped with railings to prevent the crew from falling overboard or unauthorized persons from entering. Can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments. A militarized version of the S&H design, improved with a reinforced floor and armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Armored Deck (no railings)
description: A platform that improves the trampler's ergonomics, it provides more comfortable navigation on the roofs of compartments. It can only be installed on top of load-bearing structures like chassis and solid compartments. A militarized version of the S&H design, improved with a reinforced floor. This particular model comes with the railings removed.

name: S.Trs Armored Frame
description: An additional load-bearing frame that allows the placement of decks without blocking the space below them. More lightweight than solid compartments but more fragile. A militarized version of the S&H design, improved with a reinforced floor and armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Armored Frame, Triangle
description: An additional load-bearing frame that allows the placement of decks without blocking the space below them. More lightweight than solid compartments but more fragile. A militarized version of the S&H design, improved with a reinforced floor and armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Armored Turret Deck
description: The lower support of a gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. Additionally equipped with armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Armored Turret Deck, Corner
description: The lower support of a gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. Additionally equipped with armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Auxiliary Corridor
description: A compartment that can be installed to improve the trampler's ergonomics by creating more protected passages between functional compartments. Quite fragile.

name: S.Trs Captain's Cabin
description: A crew compartment specially designed for the trampler's captain. The standard equipment includes a personal refrigerator and reinforced armor. The cabin also houses the Trampler Ownership Device, "IO". Crew access to doors and other trampler functions is granted and overwritten exclusively through this device. This modification of the compartment was designed for warships; therefore, it is equipped with additional armor.

name: S.Trs Cargo Bay, L-Shape
description: A modified version of the S&H cargo bay design, reinforced by armored walls. Even sturdier, but heavier. Was used on military cruisers to transport valuable cargo, such as weapons, spare parts, and even top-secret prototypes.

name: S.Trs Cargo Bay, Parallel
description: A modified version of the S&H cargo bay design, reinforced by armored walls. Even sturdier, but heavier. Was used on military cruisers to transport valuable cargo, such as weapons, spare parts, and even top-secret prototypes.

name: S.Trs Cargo Bay, U-Shape
description: A modified version of the S&H cargo bay design, reinforced by armored walls. Even sturdier, but heavier. Was used on military cruisers to transport valuable cargo, such as weapons, spare parts, and even top-secret prototypes.

name: S.Trs Cargo Compartment, Medium
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Has noticeably more armor compared to S&H cargo decks. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S.Trs Cargo Compartment, Small
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Has noticeably more armor compared to S&H cargo decks. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S.Trs Cargo Compartment, Small L-Shape
description: A compartment designed for transporting small loads. Has noticeably more armor compared to S&H cargo decks. Ensures that all items placed on its shelves will remain intact during Trampler Deployment and Evacuation. Can hold off a burglar but won't protect your loot from an explosion.

name: S.Trs Entrance Vestibule
description: A crucial part of any trampler. The ladder significantly eases the process of climbing onto the trampler. A fully enclosed entrance with a vestibule provides enhanced security measures. This compartment cannot be installed on top of other compartments or chassis, as the access hatch with the ladder is mounted in its lower deck.

name: S.Trs HA Armor Plate
description: Armor plate scavenged from Imperial cruisers. Strong enough to withstand a couple of hits. Sloped to improve protection and the likelihood of ricochet. Due to the design specifics, it is not subject to field repairs.

name: S.Trs HAs Framed Armor Plate
description: Armor plate scavenged from Imperial cruisers. Strong enough to withstand a couple of hits. Sloped to improve protection and the likelihood of ricochet. Due to the design specifics, it is not subject to field repairs. A frame, mounted around the armor plate, allows for another compartment to be installed above it.

name: S.Trs HAs-R Framed Armor Plate
description: Armor plate scavenged from Imperial cruisers. Strong enough to withstand a couple of hits. Sloped to improve protection and the likelihood of ricochet. Due to the design specifics, it is not subject to field repairs. This placement provides an additional angle, increasing the chance of ricochet from new directions.

name: S.Trs K81v Crew Cabin, 2 People
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days.

name: S.Trs K81v Pilot Bridge
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn! This modification has additional layers of armor and was commissioned by the Imperial Army for installation on Imperial military tramplers.

name: S.Trs K82v Pilot Bridge
description: S&H engineers have combined every trampler movement control into one module, making it a universal control point. Marching, turning, sidestep, emergency brake. They even installed a horn! This modification has additional layers of armor and was commissioned by the Imperial Army for installation on Imperial military tramplers.

name: S.Trs "Karl Ludvig" Royal Chassis
description: Specially designed by Seearsenal Neues Triest engineers for the "Karl Ludvig" Flagman Trampler of Sophie's Military Fleet. It was named after Franz Joseph's brother. It loses in maneuverability to any civilian model but can literally carry a military fort on itself.

name: S.Trs M78v Embrasure
description: The basic embrasure that was installed on most Imperial military tramplers in Sophie's military fleet. It provides decent cover for a soldier inside along with a good arc of fire to defend the trampler from approaching infantry.

name: S.Trs MB75 Crew Cabin, Single
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days. The compact model is designed for personal use.

name: S.Trs MB77e Crew Cabin, 4 People
description: These cabins were designed to sustain a trampler crew during long journeys. Every new shift starts here. Provides all the necessary means of comfort, including a refrigerator to store provisions and a ventilation system that provides improved air circulation on hot days. This particular design features increased room capacity and can sustain up to four crew members.

name: S.Trs Turret Deck
description: The lower support of the gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons.

name: S.Trs Turret Deck, Corner
description: The lower support of a gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons.

name: S.Trs Wide Armored Turret Deck
description: The lower support of a gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. The wide deck improves the working conditions of the gun crew, but takes up more space. Additionally equipped with armored railings to provide cover during gunfights.

name: S.Trs Wide Turret Deck
description: The lower support of the gun carriage, mounted on a reinforced deck. Designed to accommodate the full range of available artillery weapons. The wide deck improves the working conditions of the gun crew, but takes up more space.

name: Segen
description: This page has no content, perhaps you would like to add some?

name: Blitz 10R Pistol
description: The O'Donnel "Blitz" 8 mm semi-automatic pistol. Swift and deadly. Highly effective in close quarters. Compensates for its low accuracy with a high fire rate.

name: Blitz PPS-5 Pistol
description: The O'Donnel "Blitz" 8 mm semi-automatic pistol. Swift and deadly. A preproduction sample with a reduced capacity magazine capable of holding five rounds.

name: Blitz 15R Pistol
description: The O'Donnel "Blitz" 8 mm semi-automatic pistol. Swift and deadly. A rare modification with an increased capacity magazine, capable of holding 15 rounds. Even more bullets to miss the target with.

name: SGOW Universal casemate
description: One of the prototypes created by SGOW for the flagship Imperial cruisers. It combines the ability to install any type of artillery weapons with a protected gun mount to shield its crew from enemy shots and explosions. However, such protection comes at a price: the gun has a smaller arc of fire compared to the deck model, it costs the same as a small ship, and refuses to work without external power.

name: Pepper Mill Shotgun
description: The custom O'Donnel 12 GA shotgun that was called the "Pepper Mill" because of it rotating barrels. "Give 'Em Some Pepper!"

name: 12 GA Ammo
description: Basic buckshot ammo used by all shotguns. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 12 GA Heavy Buckshot Ammo
description: Special buckshot ammo used by all shotguns. Bigger pellets make it more effective against armor. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 12 GA Buckshot Ammo (HE)
description: Explosive buckshot ammo. Buckshot pellets are filled with an explosive mixture. Extraordinary efforts led to an impressive result. Manufactured by Kaiser's Friends.

name: 12 GA Dragon's Breath Ammo
description: Incendiary buckshot ammo. Buckshot pellets are mixed with highly flammable materials. A prototype made by Neue Zukunft.

name: 12 GA Shotgun Slug
description: Experimental ammunition that uses a single solid bullet instead of buckshot, which significantly increases lethality. Designed for hunting very large animals. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 12 GA Toxic Ammo
description: Special buckshot ammo. Buckshot pellets are mixed with highly toxic materials provided by Sophie's Molfars.

name: Drobulet Shotgun
description: A makeshift 12 GA shotgun frequently used by Kaiser's Friends. They call it a "Drobulet" between each other.

name: Drobulet Shotgun (Vertical Choke)
description: A makeshift 12 GA shotgun frequently used by Kaiser's Friends. Additionally equipped with a vertical choke to increase effectiveness against tall and thin targets.

name: Triplet Shotgun
description: O'Donnell's exclusive 12-gauge shotgun, nicknamed the "Triplet" because it fires in bursts from all three barrels at once. Blows away everything in its path (literally!).

name: 70 mm Shell
description: Canister shot that contains a high number of small projectiles. Utilized by shotgun cannons.

name: Interior Explosion 70 mm Shell
description: Explosive shell for 70 mm shotgun cannons that detonates inside the target.

name: 70 mm Shotgun Cannon Slug
description: Armor-piercing ammunition with a solid shell, designed for use with 70 mm shotgun cannons. It can't compete with 80 mm long-barreled artillery shells, but without those – beggars can't be choosers.

name: Smoke 70 mm Shell
description: A shotgun turret shell casing filled with a special mixture that, when detonated, creates a huge cloud of thick smoke. Not intended to cause damage, but can become a key tactical tool that can change the outcome of a battle. A prototype from Neue Zukunft.

name: 40 mm Shell
description: A smaller-caliber cannon shell utilized by autocannons.

name: Long-Range 40 mm Shell
description: A smaller-caliber cannon shell utilized by autocannons. Contains increased powder charge to increase range.

name: Low-Recoil 40 mm Shell
description: A 40 mm shell with a slightly altered powder formula, providing reduced recoil at the cost of a lower initial projectile velocity. Even though the projectile is slower, it still remains deadly in close- and medium-range combat encounters. Manufactured by SGOW engineers to extend the service life of older versions of the fleet's autocannons, which had significantly less efficient recoil mechanisms compared to their newer models.

name: Small Valuables
description: Some valuable knick-knacks left behind by the previous residents during the evacuation.

name: RG79s Smoke Grenade
description: A disposable source of black smoke. Whether it will be a decoy, signal, or disguise is up to you. Handle with care.

name: NZ Mk2-RF Smokeless Energy Rod
description:
A main energy source. A standardized capsule with aurogen that fits most reactors and motor-reactors.
A covert ops version that can cause the motor-reactor to stop emitting visible smoke for quite a long period of time.

name: 1874s Petros Sniper Rifle
description: The Bartka "Petros" 11 mm sniper rifle. Equipped with a telescopic sight. Every shot counts that much more when you only have one shot available.

name: 11x54 mm Ammo
description: Basic ammo used by long-range rifles. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 11x54 mm AP Ammo
description: Special armor-piercing ammo with a hardened penetrating core. Used with long-range rifles. Manufactured by SGOW.

name: 1887/2 "The Twin" Double-Barreled Rifle
description: Bartka's "The Twin" 11 mm double-barreled sniper rifle. Equipped with a telescopic sight. It's always nice to have a second chance. Even if it's rarely needed, given the power of each shot.

name: 1874e Petros Rifle
description: The Bartka "Petros" 11 mm bolt-action rifle. Simple, reliable, and very accurate. Every shot counts that much more when you only have one shot available.

name: 1874e/sd Petros Rifle (Silenced)
description: The Bartka "Petros" 11 mm bolt-action rifle. Simple, reliable, and very accurate. Equipped with a silencer. A handy tool to surprise your opponent with.

name: 1874s/sd Petros Sniper Rifle (Silenced)
description: The Bartka "Petros" 11 mm sniper rifle. Equipped with a telescopic sight and a silencer. A handy tool to surprise your opponent with.

name: Storm Dive
description: Storm Dive is one of the two game modes in Sand, after selecting your trampler, inventory and loadout, you will be dropped onto the surface of Sophie by the insertion ship. You'll have limited time to explore, fight and loot before being consumed by the ever approaching dust storm, so you'd better keep moving!

name: Suspicious Pile of Sand
description:
The Suspicious Pile of Sand is a Loot Container that can only be found at Shipwrecks. Unlike other containers, the pile only has one rarity: Normal.
It is suspected that the pile does have a rarity value, but this information is hidden from the players view unlike other containers that state their rarity in the name and by visual color differences. As such, it can only be speculated at if the container contains different drop tables or not.
The pile is similar to locked Boxes in that it requires a Key to open, that being a [[treasure-shovel]], to unearth the pile and get to whatever buried treasure it contains. Unlike locked boxes, the pile is a static object and can not be picked up.
The contents within a pile is extremely varied and comes in distinct sets. Data is still being collected on what exactly can be found but notable drops include: Armored Jacket, AV1 Rocket Launcher and Rockets, Triplet Shotgun, and KF866/9R "Mehrzel" Repeater.

name: MedKit
description: Whether it's a critical wound or a small scratch, the MedKit will fix it in no time.

name: Time Bomb
description: An incredibly serious argument that can open a lot of doors for you. Make sure to activate it before running away.

name: Rusty Shovel
description: This shovel is in terrible condition and literally falls apart in your hands. However, according to the accounting journals, it seems that funds for its repair and maintenance were regularly allocated by the foreman. Used to dig out [[suspicious-pile-of-sand]]

name: 80 mm Shell
description: A regular type of shell utilized by Naval Cannons.

name: 80 mm Proximity Fuse Shell
description: A rather unique invention consisting of a conventional 80 mm cannon shell combined with a proximity fuse. When fired, the shell explodes at a distance sufficient to damage the target, which greatly simplifies aiming – no direct hit required! Invented by some unknown engineer who definitely didn't blow himself up in the process of creating it.

name: EMP 80 mm Shell
description: One of the Neue Zukunft prototypes. The warhead of a standard 80 mm artillery shell is reinforced with stabilized aurogen crystals. Deals less damage compared to the base shell, but is capable of disabling all aurogen-based machinery in the area of impact.

name: High Velocity 80 mm Shell
description: A modification of standard artillery shells, compatible with naval cannons. Increased initial projectile velocity, made possible by a revised powder charge formula.

name: Valuable Papers
description: These papers might contain some information that can be sold to someone.

name: Valuables Safe
description:
The Valuables Safe is a Loot Container that can be found all across Sophie, most commonly inside buildings at city and port Landmarks.
Just like all containers, it has 3 "Rarity" tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare. Normal safes contain Crowns, either Small Valuables OR Valuable Papers, and Coral Chunks within. Rare and very rare safes contain additional items, those being Leviathan Skin and Optic Lenses/Crystal, respectively.
Safes also contain a hidden "Quality" tier that is primarily location driven based on where the crate spawns at.
Both the rarity and quality of a safe impact how much of each item the safe contains within.

name: Voyage
description:
Voyage is one of the two game modes in Sand, after selecting your trampler, inventory and loadout, you will be dropped onto the surface of Sophie by the insertion ship.
Once the Voyage starts you have as long as you like to explore the surface of Sophie, gather resources and engage in piracy hang out with your new found friends!
Once you're ready to leave, you'll have to head to one of the Extraction Points scattered around Sophie, and use the radio placed at the top of the tower to engage the extraction

name: Weapon Crate
description: The Weapon Crate is a Loot Container which mainly stores Player Weapons, Ammunition for player weapons, and Raw Materials like Scrapped Ammo. Just like all containers, it has 3 tiers: Normal, Rare, and Very Rare. it can be found all across Sophie.

name: Crate of 1889 Chardonnay
description: Wine from Sophie's best vineyards. It is well bought by snobs in Vienna.

name: E-Wok Bomb
description: A sea mine cooked up according to a secret Molfar recipe, with the addition of local coral dust, a pinch of aurogen, and a countdown clock. Served inactivated. Once activated, an explosion is inevitable. Extremely powerful!
